results.scenario_name: Scenario 21 — Multi-Parcel Tracking Partial Delivery Detection
results.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Shipment and Parcel clearly distinguished as aggregate root and child entity with independent lifecycles. (2) BR-3 explicitly states shipment status 'MUST be derived from the aggregate of its Parcel statuses. It must never be set independently' — the critical derivation insight is front and center. (3) 9+ parcel-level states defined (Created, Picked Up, In Transit, Out for Delivery, Delivered, Damaged, Lost, Delivery Failed, Returned to Sender, Cancelled). (4) Business invariants cover partial delivery detection (BR-4), lost parcel escalation (BR-10), and parcel-level dispute granularity (BR-8). (5) 8 non-trivial edge cases including out-of-order events, split delivery addresses, damaged parcel replacement, legacy migration, and dispute timing. (6) 10 detailed acceptance criteria in Given/When/Then format.

observations.text: BR-3 and BR-4 together nail the derivation rule with explicit status mapping logic and a testable assertion — this is the highest-value domain insight and Bird made it the centerpiece
Ubiquitous language section is exceptionally well-defined with 9 terms including Terminal State and Parcel Exception — shows deep domain thinking
Edge case for out-of-order tracking events (network partition) addresses a real distributed systems concern in logistics

Escalations section identifies three genuine ambiguities (dispute resolution outcomes, replacement parcel handling, lost detection trigger) with concrete options and recommendations — goes beyond analysis into actionable PM decisions
AC-8 covers legacy migration as a single-parcel wrapper, showing awareness of backward compatibility concerns
Business impact section quantifies financial, operational, and user impact with specific stakeholder analysis — unusual depth for a domain analysis
BR-7 mandates immutable event history per parcel — correctly identifies audit trail as a hard constraint in logistics
Could have explicitly modeled DeliveryAttempt as a value object rather than just referencing it in BR-6 — the entity decomposition stops at Parcel level
Time window for partial delivery escalation (how long to wait before notifying customer of partial delivery) is not addressed as a business rule or edge case

meta.notes: Preliminary scoring by Coach K. Human review pending via web app at localhost:3000.
